title: gh-134744: Fix fcntl error handling
author: vstinner
body:
<!--
Thanks for your contribution!
Please read this comment in its entirety. It's quite important.

# Pull Request title

It should be in the following format:

```
gh-NNNNNN: Summary of the changes made
```

Where: gh-NNNNNN refers to the GitHub issue number.

Most PRs will require an issue number. Trivial changes, like fixing a typo, do not need an issue.

# Backport Pull Request title

If this is a backport PR (PR made against branches other than `main`),
please ensure that the PR title is in the following format:

```
[X.Y] <title from the original PR> (GH-NNNNNN)
```

Where: [X.Y] is the branch name, for example: [3.13].

GH-NNNNNN refers to the PR number from `main`.

-->


<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-134744 -->
* Issue: gh-134744
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
@serhiy-storchaka: It seems like only the main branch is affected.

I wrote a patch adding tests but it doesn't check modified error paths:

```py
diff --git a/Lib/test/test_fcntl.py b/Lib/test/test_fcntl.py
index e0e6782258f..e1c72c6ab7e 100644
--- a/Lib/test/test_fcntl.py
+++ b/Lib/test/test_fcntl.py
@@ -11,7 +11,7 @@
     cpython_only, get_pagesize, is_apple, requires_subprocess, verbose
 )
 from test.support.import_helper import import_module
-from test.support.os_helper import TESTFN, unlink
+from test.support.os_helper import TESTFN, unlink, make_bad_fd


 # Skip test if no fcntl module.
@@ -274,6 +274,13 @@ def test_fcntl_small_buffer(self):
     def test_fcntl_large_buffer(self):
         self._check_fcntl_not_mutate_len(2024)

+    @unittest.skipUnless(hasattr(fcntl, 'FICLONE'), 'need fcntl.FICLONE')
+    def test_bad_fd(self):
+        # gh-134744: Test error handling
+        fd = make_bad_fd()
+        with self.assertRaises(OSError):
+            fcntl.ioctl(fd, fcntl.F_DUPFD, 0)
+

 if __name__ == '__main__':
     unittest.main()
diff --git a/Lib/test/test_ioctl.py b/Lib/test/test_ioctl.py
index 3c7a58aa2bc..e2f94dbf5dd 100644
--- a/Lib/test/test_ioctl.py
+++ b/Lib/test/test_ioctl.py
@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@
 import threading
 import unittest
 from test import support
-from test.support import threading_helper
+from test.support import os_helper, threading_helper
 from test.support.import_helper import import_module
 fcntl = import_module('fcntl')
 termios = import_module('termios')
@@ -201,6 +201,15 @@ def test_ioctl_set_window_size(self):
         new_winsz = struct.unpack("HHHH", result)
         self.assertEqual(new_winsz[:2], (20, 40))

+    @unittest.skipUnless(hasattr(fcntl, 'FICLONE'), 'need fcntl.FICLONE')
+    def test_bad_fd(self):
+        # gh-134744: Test error handling
+        with open(__file__) as fp:
+            fd1 = fp.fileno()
+            fd2 = os_helper.make_bad_fd()
+            with self.assertRaises(OSError):
+                fcntl.ioctl(fd1, fcntl.FICLONE, fd2)
+

 if __name__ == "__main__":
     unittest.main()
```

comments.author: sergey-miryanov
comments.body: @vstinner It seems that on 139 and 321 lines it may leak.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
> @vstinner It seems that on 139 and 321 lines it may leak.

Would you mind to elaborate? What leak? In which file?

comments.author: sergey-miryanov
comments.body:
Sorry, I was not clear.

In `fcntlmodule.c`

https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3c0525126ef95efe2f578e93db09f3282e3ca08f/Modules/fcntlmodule.c#L130-L141
As you can see `result` decrefed on 134, but not on 139 lines.

https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3c0525126ef95efe2f578e93db09f3282e3ca08f/Modules/fcntlmodule.c#L312-L323
Same here - it decrefed on 316, but not on 321.

comments.author: serhiy-storchaka
comments.body:
Thank you @vstinner. The code is the same in 3.14 and main, but 3.13 is not affected. It is my fail.

For testing you need to pass a large bytes object (more than 1024 bytes). Simply pass `b'\0' * 2048`, it should fail with bad file descriptor.

Thank you @sergey-miryanov for noticing a leak.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: Ok, I added tests and I fixed the reference leak spotted by @sergey-miryanov.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: @serhiy-storchaka: Would you mind to review my fix?

comments.author: vstinner
serhiy-storchaka
comments.body: Ah, so both bugs was introduced while adding a buffer overflow check.

comments.author: serhiy-storchaka
comments.body: It would be nice to backport the tests.

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body: [GH-134795](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/134795) is a backport of this pull request to the [3.14 branch](https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/3.14).

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body:
> It would be nice to backport the tests.

I wrote https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/134795 to backport tests. I had to modify tests since Python 3.14 only supports buffer up to 1024 bytes.
